name: Circular Els Tres Monts des de Mura
name_original: Fuentes y Cuevas de Mura, Puig de la Balma – Ruta Circular
description: Hike the Fuentes y Cuevas de Mura, Puig de la Balma Circular Route, a moderate 5.4-mile trail through Mura's caves and springs.
region_intro: Hiking around Mura offers diverse terrain within the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac, characterized by its mountainous landscape, forests, and unique rock formations. The region features a network of trails that traverse hills and valleys, providing varied elevation changes. Natural features include ancient woodlands, rocky outcrops, and scenic viewpoints overlooking the surrounding area. Best hiking trails around Mura The most popular hiking route is Circular Els Tres Monts des de Mura, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete, offering a moderate challenge through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hospital de Sang and Turó de la Cort Fosca Circular Trail, a moderate 3.9 miles (6.3 km) path, winding through forests and past historical sites. Local hikers also love the Mura – Puig de la Balma loop from Mura, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Hiking around Mura is defined by mountainous terrain, dense woodlands, and scenic viewpoints, with options for different ability levels from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Mura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 330 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Mura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Mura?
A: Mura offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 120 routes. These trails cater to different skill levels, including 2 easy routes, 68 moderate routes, and 53 more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain and natural features can I expect while hiking in Mura?
A: Hiking in Mura primarily takes you through the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac. The terrain is mountainous, featuring dense woodlands, unique rock formations, and varied elevation changes. You'll encounter ancient forests, rocky outcrops, and scenic viewpoints overlooking the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners in Mura?
A: Yes, Mura has several easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5333386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mura – Puig de la Balma loop from Mura&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.1-mile (5.0 km) trail that can be completed in under two hours, offering a pleasant journey through the local countryside.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Mura?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in Mura. A notable example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950011727/circular-els-tres-monts-des-de-mura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Els Tres Monts des de Mura&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.4-mile (8.7 km) trail that offers a moderate challenge through varied landscapes. Another favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938985952/hospital-de-sang-and-turo-de-la-cort-fosca-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hospital de Sang and Turó de la Cort Fosca Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.9-mile (6.3 km) path winding through forests and past historical sites.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to see along the trails?
A: Mura's trails are rich with natural and historical landmarks. You can explore various peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/423435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola and the Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/423431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcau Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features interesting caves, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1588289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Blood Hospital&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2857414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cau dels Emboscats Cave&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique exploration opportunities.

Q: Is Mura a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Mura offers several routes suitable for families, particularly the easier and moderate trails. The diverse landscape and points of interest like caves and viewpoints can make for an engaging experience for children. Always check the specific trail's length and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Mura?
A: Many trails in the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other hikers, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas where restrictions might apply.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mura?
A: The best time to hike in Mura is generally during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November) when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summers can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters offer a different, often quieter, experience, though some higher trails might be colder.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Mura?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Mura, with an impressive average score of 4.6 stars from over 330 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's varied terrain, the beauty of its dense woodlands, and the rewarding scenic viewpoints. Over 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Mura's trails, highlighting its popularity.

Q: Are there any trails in Mura that feature waterfalls?
A: While the primary natural features of Mura are its mountainous terrain, forests, and rock formations, the broader region of Catalonia, where Mura is located, does have areas known for waterfalls. For trails specifically featuring waterfalls, you might need to explore slightly further afield from the immediate Mura area, as the local guide data does not explicitly list waterfalls within the immediate hiking routes.

Q: What are some of the more challenging hikes in the Mura region?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Mura offers several difficult routes. One such option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10487905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola (1,103 m) – Morral del Drac loop from Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.6 km trail with significant elevation changes. Another challenging route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1472758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcau - La Mola – Simanya Petita Cave loop from Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.6 km but rated difficult due to its terrain.

Q: Are there options for public transport to reach the trailheads in Mura?
A: While Mura is a small village, public transport options to reach the area exist, though they might require some planning. It's advisable to check local bus schedules from larger nearby towns or cities that connect to Mura. For specific trailhead access, a combination of public transport and a short walk or taxi might be necessary.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Mura?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Mura, particularly near popular trailheads or designated parking areas for the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac. It's recommended to arrive early, especially on weekends or holidays, as spaces can fill up quickly. Always look for official parking signs and respect local regulations.
tips:
Festival of Mura's Uncle
Mura is a small medieval town in Barcelona in the Bages region. It is located within the Natural Park of Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac. At Christmas they celebrate the Tió de Mura Festival, which lasts several weeks and consists of the neighbors bringing out their most creative side by designing tiós and displaying them in the street for visitors to see. The Tió de Mura Festival will be held from December 6, 2019 to January 5, 2020. You can find the full program on the website of the Mura Town Hall.

Apart from strolling through its narrow streets and enjoying the local gastronomy, I recommend leaving the town and contemplating the numerous fountains that are around it (font del foradot, font del formatget, ...)

The Font del Formatget is one of the closest to Mura. You have to leave the parish church of Sant Martí, passing the Nespres stream and continue along a path that goes around some orchards near the Reixac stream. In a short distance is the fountain - it is located under the road and the water comes from a tube that falls directly to the ground.

The fountain is located in the Reixac stream, inside an espluga and next to an irrigation pond formed by the accumulation of water from the fountain. It is located in an area of crops, orchard terraces, to the south of the town center and very close to it. The cavity where the fountain is located has calcareous formations caused by the continuous effect of water, with the presence of plant species typical of humid places, mainly mosses and ferns.

The Mura caves are a single gallery about two hundred meters deep. Discovered in the middle of the last century by a peasant who, according to legend, observed how a bird entered and left some brambles that hid his access. From the Natural Park of the Serra and the Obac, guided routes are organized to be able to enter the cave every Saturday, Sunday and holidays from 10:45 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. It is necessary to book in advance. It is necessary to wear a headlamp, good footwear and water. In summer it is necessary to protect yourself from the sun, and all year round at least a warm sweater inside the cave, which is at a temperature of 12ºC.
Balma where a hostel and restaurant have been built with brutal charm

In one corner is the Font with a long seat next to it. Everything is made of stones. The outlet of the water comes out through an iron peak, the water is directed to the raft that is in front of it, and an old ditch takes the water towards the orchards.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this hike?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts from the charming medieval village of Mura. You should find parking available within or near the village, which serves as the main access point for trails in the area.

faqs.question: Is this circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trails around Mura are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other hikers, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are ideal for hiking this route, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be challenging due to heat, especially on ascents and descents, so if you go then, start early and carry plenty of water. Winter can also be lovely, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially muddy sections.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this circular route in the Natural Park of Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring mostly accessible paths, mountain hiking paths, and some unpaved sections. You will encounter rocky areas, zig-zag paths, and sections traversing streams and forests of holm oaks.

faqs.question: What notable sights can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in natural and historical highlights. You'll pass through the picturesque village of Mura, see the unique Puig de la Balma (an 11th-century farmhouse built into a rock), and explore various natural springs like Cheese Spring and Foradot Spring, as well as the Caves of Mura. Keep an eye out for the Tió de Mura Sculptures in the village during certain times of the year.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered moderate, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness due to ascents, descents, and varied terrain. While a shorter, family-friendly version exists, the full circular route might be challenging for absolute beginners, especially on warm days.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, the starting point, Mura village, offers amenities including cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your hike. Puig de la Balma also functions as a rural accommodation and restaurant.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find yourself on sections of the Ruta del romànic VII.2 - Les Valls del Montcau, Els Tres Monts, Cova de Mura, and Balmes, masies i molins, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for ascents/descents, sturdy hiking shoes are essential. Bring plenty of water, especially on warm days, as well as snacks. A map or GPS device is recommended, and if you plan to explore the Caves of Mura, you might need to arrange for keys from the Interpretation Center.

faqs.question: Are there any natural pools or swimming spots along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route features many springs and streams, natural pools like Gorg del Pare can sometimes be found. However, water levels can vary significantly depending on recent rainfall, so don't count on them always being suitable for swimming.
